bibdata.title: The International System of Units
Recommended values of standard frequencies for applications including the practical realization of the metre and secondary representations of the second

Strontium 87 Atom (
f ≈ 429 ⁢
THz
f ~~ 429 "unitsml(THz)")
Part 2.20
Appendix 2
Le système international d’unités
Valeurs recommandées des fréquences étalons destinées à la mise en pratique de la définition du mètre et aux représentations secondaires de la seconde

Strontium 87 Atom (
f ≈ 429 ⁢
THz
f ~~ 429 "unitsml(THz)")
Partie 2.20
Appendice 2

Pavillon de Breteuil
F-92312 Sèvres Cedex
FRANCE

p.strong:
87Sr neutral atom,
5
s 2
1
S 0
− 5 s 5 p
3
P 0
5"s"^2 " "^1"S"_0 - 5"s"5"p" " "^3"P"_0 unperturbed optical transition

clause.title: Recommended value of the frequency in the CIPM List of Frequencies
clause.p:
with a relative standard uncertainty of
4 ×
10
− 16
4 xx 10^(−16).
This radiation was endorsed by the CIPM as a secondary representation of the definition of the second .

clause.title: Source data
clause.p:
The adopted value of
f
(
87
Sr )
= 429228004229873.0 ⁢
Hz
f(""^{87}"Sr") = 429228004229873.0 "unitsml(Hz)" with a relative standard uncertainty of
4 ×
10
− 16
4 xx 10^(−16)

has been calculated by the methods presented in , , , , with the data from the table of frequencies (2017) attached below. The recommended uncertainty is the same as that of the most accurate comparison between the Cs Primary Frequency Standards at LNE-SYRTE and PTB via an optical fibre link i.e.
4 ×
10
− 16
4 xx 10^(−16).

fn.p:
From the least square procedure it turned out that the value and the uncertainty given in ref. is not compatible with the remaining data. Thus the uncertainty given in the original publication was increased to
3 ⁢
Hz
3 "unitsml(Hz)" to make it statistically more consistent.

The fractional uncertainty of
2.3 ×
10
− 17
2.3 xx 10^{−17} in ref. has been increased by a factor of three since this uncertainty is an order of magnitude smaller than the other input data for this transition.

L. Robertsson: On the evaluation of ultra-high-precision frequency ratio measurements: examining closed loops in a graph theory framework; Metrologia 53, 1272 (2016)

10
− 15
